title: Alya Aldrete
content:

{{Quote|Lady Aldrete controls an information network that stretches from Alderaan to Empress Teta. Her smugglers double as observers and spies, particularly in the Deep Core. Even the Bothan SpyNet can't match her infiltration of that region.|Notru'denn|<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />}}
[[Lady/Legends|Lady]] '''Alya Aldrete''' was an [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. Heiress to the business enterprise of the wealthy [[Aldrete family]] in the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Alya achieved renown and celebrity status owing to her hedonistic lifestyle. Her public persona was however just a facade; Aldrete covertly established a [[Alya Aldrete's smuggler fleet|smuggler fleet]] operating in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] regions, using her criminal contacts to gather information and map reliable [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]].

Circa [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], Aldrete attended the launch event on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] for her family's new [[Gem of Alderaan|luxury hovertrain]]. The [[Hovertrain/Legends|train]] fell under [[Battle of the Gem of Alderaan|attack]] from [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and Aldrete was briefly captured, before being rescued by agents of [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], Alderaan's [[Senator/Legends|delegate]] to the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]]. Aldrete subsequently pledged her support to Organa's [[Alderaanian Resistance]] rebel group and marshalled her smuggler [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] for a [[Battle of Byss|raid]] by the rebels against a [[Super Star Destroyer prototype|prototype Super Star Destroyer]] under [[Sarlacc Project|construction]] by the Empire in the [[Beshqek system]]. Aldrete and her forces successfully destroyed the leviathan [[Warship/Legends|warship]], achieving one of the first victories of an insurgent group against the Imperial regime.

==Biography==
===Celebrity heiress===
{{Quote|This is Lady Alya Aldrete, heiress to the Aldrete family empire of luxury hotels, casinos, passenger liners, and other playgrounds for the idle rich. Alya herself uses her family name and fortune to pursue a life of decadence and excess, making herself into a celebrity and icon while the gossip media watches her every move.|Bail Organa|<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />}}
[[Lady/Legends|Lady]] Alya Aldrete was a wealthy<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|Aldrete, Alya|I|19}}</ref> [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="Primer" /> [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> [[Noble/Legends|noblewoman]]<ref name="Primer" /> of the affluent [[Aldrete family]]<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> who lived during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]].<ref name="Primer" /> The heiress to her family's chain of [[Casino/Legends|casinos]], [[Hotel/Legends|hotels]] and [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger liners]], Aldrete rose to prominence within [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] society as a result of her excessive pleasure-seeking and debauchery. She became a celebrity icon and her activities were widely reported in the media, including details of her romantic partners, her vacations and where she went [[Nightclub/Legends|clubbing]].<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

In truth, Aldrete's public persona was just a carefully-cultivated cover.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> She formed a [[Alya Aldrete's smuggler fleet|network]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] operating in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] regions of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]],<ref name="CSWE" /> spanning from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to the world of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]], smuggling diverse goods ranging from stolen artworks to [[Spice/Legends|spice]] and other illicit luxury commodities. She kept her operation concealed from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> and used her network to chart dependable [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]]<ref name="CSWE" /> and gather information. Her infiltration of the Deep Core region exceeded that of the regarded [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]]<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] organization.<ref name="Furry and Fury">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20041216b|text=Furry and Fury}}</ref> She also employed the elderly [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] male [[Notru'denn]] as her [[Aide/Legends|personal assistant]].<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

===Launch of the ''Gem of Alderaan''===
{{Quote|Okay, everyone, I know a lot of you. And a lot of you are here because you know my grandpa. And a few are here for the free drinks. Either way, thanks for coming to our launch, and you'll be the first to see all the great stuff Grandpa put in. And there are bars in every repulsorcar and a nightclub on the back of the train—those were my ideas! And I got bouncers and everything to keep the worst riffraff outta my club—and to keep the best riffraff in!|Alya Aldrete, a the ''Gem of Alderaan'' launch celebration|<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />}}
Circa [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Echoes of the Jedi]],'' the fourth installment of the ''[[Dawn of Defiance]]'' campaign, states that the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vhiin Thorla]] traveled to the [[Almas Academy]] two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the declaration of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which is dated to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. This thus indicates that ''Dawn of Defiance'' is set in circa [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].</ref> Alya's grandfather commissioned a new addition to the Aldrete family business empire, the luxury,<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> high-speed [[Hovertrain/Legends|hovertrain]] ''[[Gem of Alderaan]]'',<ref name="Primer" /> and the socialite took an active role in the [[Train/Legends|train's]] design; at her suggestion [[Cantina/Legends|bars]] were placed in every hovercar on the train. The rear car of the hovertrain was designated as Aldrete's personal hovercar and housed the [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclub]], [[Decadence nightclub|Decadence]]. The club's ambience embodied Aldrete's reputation for self-indulgence and vice, and she employed several [[Bouncer (occupation)/Legends|bouncers]] to prevent those who she deemed sufficiently important or attractive from entering the venue. A personal office for Aldrete was also installed, located on the floor above the club.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

An exclusive launch party was staged to mark the maiden journey of the new train,<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> a [[Standard hour/Legends|six-hour]] trip departing from the Alderaan city [[Aldera/Legends|Aldera]], with Lady Aldrete attending as the guest of honor.<ref name="Primer" /> At the end of reception gala held<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> at an Alderaan<ref name="Primer" /> [[Train station/Legends|repulsor-station]],<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> to commence the celebration Aldrete gave a welcome speech to the gathered dignitaries. Sipping on a snifter on [[Alderaanian brandy]], she slurred her words as she spoke and invited those present to board the train. Her staff then ushered her into the ''Gem of Alderaan'' ahead of the guests and she spent the opening stages of the journey surrounded by various hangers-on. A group of agents of the [[Alderaanian Resistance]], a rebel group directed by Alderaan's [[Senator/Legends|representative]] to the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], infiltrated the train and sought an audience with Aldrete. Having received word of Aldrete's extensive connections in the Deep Core, the rebels desired her assistance with securing them passage to the planet [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]] to rescue the captive [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Denia]] from the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Imperial Inquisitor]] [[Valin Draco]]. Several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] into the journey, Notru'denn meet with the operatives on Aldrete's behalf and after he had ascertained the purpose of their mission, she agreed to speak with the agents in her office above the Decadence nightclub.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

{{RPG|''[[The Gem of Alderaan]]''}}
[[File:BailOrganaAROW.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Bail Organa]]
Before Aldrete and Organa's operatives could connect, the ''Gem of Alderaan'' fell under [[Battle of the Gem of Alderaan|assault]] from a band of [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. The Trandoshans sought to apprehend Bail Organa and incorrectly believed the senator to be a guest on the hovertrain. Aldrete was seized by the attackers; bound and gagged, she was taken to the hovertrain's [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] and held hostage by the Trandoshans' mercenary [[Commander/Legends|commander]]. The rebel agents fought off the Trandoshans and endeavored to free the noblewoman. While using Aldrete as a Human shield, the mercenary commander dragged her to the roof of the train, attached a [[Power harpoon launcher|tow cable]] to her and attempted to have his prisoner airlifted out to a [[ILH-KK Citadel-class civilian cruiser|Citadel cruiser]] overhead. However, Organa's operatives defeated the Trandoshan and freed Aldrete. Caught in a fierce wind, Aldrete almost blew off the roof but was saved by one of her rescuers just in [[Time/Legends|time]]. Moments later, shortly before the now out-of-control train careered into a [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]], Aldrete and the agents were saved by the operatives' ally [[Sirona Okeefe]], who swooped down to the roof of the stricken hovertrain in the [[Baudo-class star yacht|''Baudo''-class star yacht]] [[Banshee (Baudo-class)|''Banshee'']] and flew the group to safety.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />
{{Endgame}}

Okeefe transported Aldrete and the agents to Bail Organa's personal Alderaan retreat. Aldrete expressed her thanks to the operatives for rescuing her and agreed to aid their [[Mission to Prakith (Imperial Period)|mission]] to free Master Denia. She offered to share with them security clearances for travel to the Deep Core and also to provide the latest [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogational]] data needed to reach Prakith and a pirated [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|transceiver]] capable of sending encrypted communications over Imperial. Aldrete additionally proffered the latest intelligence from her network on the movements of Valin Draco, disclosing that Draco's [[Star Courier/Legends|Star Courier]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] had landed at the [[Citadel Inquisitorius]] Imperial stronghold on Prakith [[Standard month/Legends|months]] ago and according to intercepted messages he was holding Denia captive there. Aldrete then parted ways with her rescuers and promised to share any intelligence useful for their mission to Prakith that came available to her and to contact them regarding the supply of the rigged transceiver.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

===Raid on the Super Star Destroyer prototype===
{{Quote|While you were traveling to Prakith, I sent out the call for all of my contacts in the Deep Core to come to me, and one of them brought some very timely data. We have not only discovered the location of the Sarlacc Project above the planet Byss, but we have also obtained a full readout of the vessel's defenders, and we know that the Sarlacc Project, while not yet complete, is within weeks of being so. What I have proposed to my captains is a mission to destroy the prototype.|Alya Aldrete|<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />}}
Aldrete joined the cause of the Alderaanian rebels, supporting their [[Sarlacc Project campaign|campaign]] to thwart the [[Sarlacc Project|construction]] of a [[Super Star Destroyer prototype|prototype Super Star Destroyer]] by the Empire<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" /> and covertly used her riches to assist the rebel movement.<ref name="CSWE" /> She made good on her offer of forged credentials for transit to Prakith for the team of operatives<ref name="Sword of the Empire">{{DoD|Sword of the Empire}}</ref> who rescued her on the ''Gem of Alderaan''<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> and the agents departed for the Deep Core world to find Denia.<ref name="Sword of the Empire" /> At the same time, Aldrete summoned all of her contacts in the Deep Core to assemble in preparation for a raid against the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] prototype construction site, in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the Imperial [[Throneworld/Legends|throneworld]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] in the [[Beshqek system]]. Her smuggler [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] mustered at a rendezvous point next to a large [[Gas/Legends|gas]] [[Cloud/Legends|cloud]] within the boundaries of the restricted [[Deep Core Security Zone]] and Aldrete's agents brought to her news that the prototype [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was only weeks away from completion.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />

[[File:Sarlaccproject.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Super Star Destroyer prototype]]
Aldrete's fleet was joined by the surviving vessels of an Alderaanian Resistance [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] that had been crippled by an Imperial [[Battle of the Resurgence|attack]]<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" /> several months earlier.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> Aldrete designated a modified [[DP20 gunship/Legends|Corellian Gunship]] as her [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and, working with Bail Organa, she devised her battle plan. Judging her armada for to be insufficiently strong to [[Space warfare/Legends|cripple]] both the Super Star Destroyer prototype and the Imperial fleet defending it, she propositioned to the [[Captain/Legends|captains]] of her smuggler vessels that they were to draw out the prototypes' ancillary fleet while Alderaan Resistance saboteurs boarded a [[Golan space defense platform|Golan space platform]] protecting the prototype and used it to cripple the Star Destroyer. Organa also informed her of the outcome of the rescue mission to Prakith, revealing that Master Denia had fallen during the attempt to free her.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />

Lady Aldrete selected the rebel operatives who saved her from the Trandoshan mercenaries to conduct the infiltration of the Golan platform. She invited the agents to dock their vessel with her flagship and called them to the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] to brief them personally on their assignment. The rebel fleet departed for the Beshqek [[Star system/Legends|system]] and Aldrete led her forces into battle and engaged the prototype's protective fleet. Her attack plan soon went awry; the under-construction Super Star Destroyer was more complete than she had anticipated and [[Precision hyperspace jump|microjumped]] into the midst of the fight and opened fire on the smuggler forces. Her strike force overwhelmed by the prototype's firepower, Aldrete commanded her armada to assume a defensive formation.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />

{{RPG|''[[Jaws of the Sarlacc]]''}}
As a final gambit to clutch victory from seeming defeat, Aldrete's tactical advisers proposed that a formation of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] fly into a gap in the Star Destroyer's [[Hull/Legends|fuselage]] and detonate the vessel's [[Reactor/Legends|main reactor]]. Aldrete entreated the team of operatives who saved her on Alderaan to assume command of [[Blue Squadron (Alderaanian Resistance)|Blue Squadron]], the unit chosen for this high-risk assignment. The starfighters successfully entered the Super Star Destroyer and blew up the reactor, causing the gargantuan prototype warship to explode; the smuggler fleet then withdrew into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The success of Aldrete and her forces<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" /> in destroying the Star Destroyer represented one of the first victories of a rebel group against the Galactic Empire.<ref name="Primer" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Lady Aldrete maintains the image of a spoiled heiress because it keeps the Empire away. The gossip media constantly reports on where she's clubbing, who she's dating, and where she's vacationing, so why assign an Imperial Intelligence agent to do the same?|Notru'denn|<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />}}
Alya Aldrete posed within society as a spoilt, fun-loving self-centered noblewoman who used her family's wealth to engage in a hedonistic and decadent life style. When playing the part of her adopted persona she acted in an erratic and attention-seeking manner, seeking to garner significant media coverage. She happily gave speeches in public while seemingly intoxicated and adopting an informal manner, and she espoused elitist views, demeaning beings outside of the wealthy elite as "riffraff". In reality, her celebrity façade was just an act to mask her criminal network; Aldrete reasoned that the Empire would not send its [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agency to investigate the activities of debauched celebrity whose movements were widely reported by the press.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

Resourceful<ref name="Sword of the Empire" /> and focused, Aldrete's true self was intelligent and serious. She adopting a professional tone when conducting business, jarringly in contrast with her irreverent public personality. Aldrete was grateful when Bail Organa's agents saved her from the Trandoshans that raided the ''Gem of Alderaan''<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> and became committed to supporting the operatives' with rescuing Master Denia.<ref name="Sword of the Empire" /> She came to consider the agents her friends and felt deep sympathy for them following the loss of Denia on Prakith.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />

The heiress was determined in her opposition to the Galactic Empire and considered her effort to destroy the Super Star Destroyer prototype to be for benefit of the galaxy. By the time of the battle in the Beshqek system, her campaign had left her tired and fearful, and she respected the loyalty of those who served in her armada during the raid.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />

Aldrete had [[Color/Legends|fair]] skin, blonde [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. During the ''Gem of Alderaan'' launch event she wore a low-cut revealing [[Clothing/Legends|dress]] of a style not typically worn by nobility.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Well, it would come to this, wouldn't it? Trapped by the Empire, with all of these loyal men and women crushed by its might. What we do now, we do for the good of the galaxy. Good luck, my friends. See to it that everyone knows that the Empire can be brought low—we merely failed ''today''.|Lady Alya Aldrete, before sacrificing herself if the player characters of ''Jaws of the Sarlacc'' failed to damage the Super Star Destroyer prototype|<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />}}
Alya Aldrete featured as a character in ''[[Dawn of Defiance]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] campaign set within [[Star Wars Legends]] continuity and released on [[Wizards.com]] as a supplement to the [[Saga Edition]] of [[Wizards of the Coast]]s' [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]. She was first mentioned in ''[[Dawn of Defiance: Gamemaster Primer]]'', a synopsis of the ''Dawn of Defiance'' campaign by [[Rodney Thompson]], published on [[October 4]], [[2007]],<ref name="Primer" /> and made her first appearance in the eighth adventure of the campaign, ''[[The Gem of Alderaan]]'' by [[Gary M. Sarli]], released on [[February 5]] [[2009]],<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> Later that year, she also featured in the final instalment of the series, ''[[Jaws of the Sarlacc]]'' by [[Rodney Thompson]].<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" /> Alya shares her surname with the [[Star Wars]] Legends characters [[Agrippa Aldrete/Legends|Agrippa Aldrete]]<ref name="Episode I Visual Dictionary">''[[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> and [[Celana Aldrete]],<ref name="Dark Lord">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref> though no family relationship between Alya and those individuals has been confirmed in any source.

In the ''Dawn of Defiance'' series, the players roleplay as the band of Alderaanian Resistance agents that Aldrete interacts with on Alderaan<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> and during the battle to destroy the prototype Star Destroyer.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" /> In ''Gem of Alderaan'', when the players attempt to rescue Aldrete from her Trandoshan captor the manner in which encounter plays out is dependent on the players' actions; the Trandoshan mercenary commander uses her as a Human shield and attempts to have her airlifted out unless stopped by the rebel operatives. It is also possible for strong winds to blow her off the roof of the hovertrain unless the player characters catch her beforehand.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" />

In ''Jaws of the Sarlacc'', if the player characters fail to use the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] of the Golan platform defending the prototype to tear a gap in the Star Destroyer's superstructure, Aldrete sacrifices herself to create a hole to allow access to the vessel's interior. Aldrete sends a communication to the player characters to thank them and ask that they spread the word that the Empire can be defeated, then directs her Corellian Gunship to speed into the side of the prototype, resulting in her [[Death/Legends|death]]. If the players did succeed at damaging the Super Star Destroyer with the platform's tractor beam, Aldrete does not sacrifice herself in such a manner and she requests that the player characters lead a starfighter squadron into the interior of the warship. If Aldrete falls, then it is the Alderaanian [[Officer/Legends|officer]] Captain [[Adrian Verana]], not Aldrete, that requests the player characters lead the mission, otherwise it is the Alderaan heiress that engages the assistance of the players for the mission.<ref name="Jaws of the Sarlacc" />
